body:
I think this is needed after #5604?

Closes #5641

On this PR:

```Julia
julia> using Oceananigans

julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(CPU(); size=(20, 30, 4), x=(-10, 10), y=(-10, 10), z=(-10, 0), halo=(6, 6, 5))
20×30×4 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 6×6×5 halo
├── Periodic x ∈ [-10.0, 10.0) regularly spaced with Δx=1.0
├── Periodic y ∈ [-10.0, 10.0) regularly spaced with Δy=0.666667
└── Bounded  z ∈ [-10.0, 0.0]  regularly spaced with Δz=2.5

julia> model = HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel(grid;
                                           free_surface = SplitExplicitFreeSurface(grid; substeps = 5),
                                           closure = CATKEVerticalDiffusivity())
HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel{CPU, RectilinearGrid}(time = 0 seconds, iteration = 0)
├── grid: 20×30×4 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 6×6×5 halo
├── timestepper: QuasiAdamsBashforth2TimeStepper
├── tracers: e
├── closure: CATKEVerticalDiffusivity{VerticallyImplicitTimeDiscretization}
├── buoyancy: Nothing
├── free surface: SplitExplicitFreeSurface with gravitational acceleration 9.80665 m s⁻²
│   └── substepping: FixedSubstepNumber(3)
├── advection scheme: 
│   ├── momentum: VectorInvariant
│   └── e: Centered(order=2)
├── vertical_coordinate: ZCoordinate
└── coriolis: Nothing

julia> Oceananigans.Diagnostics.DiffusiveCFL(0.1)(model)
0.0
```

while on `#main` it breaks; see #5641.
title: Update `Diagnostics.cell_diffusion_timescale` method for vertically-implicit treatment
